entities.name: CASH CROPPING
entities.description: The practice of growing crops for sale rather than for subsistence.

entities.name: DEFORESTATION
entities.description: The clearing of forest area, often associated with agricultural expansion.

entities.name: RICE
entities.description: The specific cash crop analyzed for its association with deforestation.

entities.name: BOLIVIAN AMAZON
entities.description: The geographical region from which empirical data was collected.

entities.name: DAILY INCOME LEVEL
entities.description: A target financial metric of $1 per person per day derived from cash cropping.

entities.name: WORK REQUIREMENTS
entities.description: The amount of labor needed for crop cultivation.

entities.name: LABOR AVAILABILITY
entities.description: The amount of labor available within a household.

entities.name: FALLOWS DURATION
entities.description: The period during which land is left unseeded to restore fertility.

entities.name: PRODUCTIVITY
entities.description: The efficiency of production, which can mitigate the need for further deforestation.

entities.name: DIVERSIFICATION OF INCOME SOURCES
entities.description: The strategy of having multiple sources of income to reduce reliance on a single crop.

relationships.description: doubling area sown increases forest cleared by 26-30 percent
cultivation used to reach a target income level
can lead to requirements exceeding availability
increasing productivity helps avoid increase in deforestation
diversification helps avoid increase in deforestation
